programme.title: Gatra Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: Gatra Beach conservation is one of the remote destinations in nature that has much to offer.

programme.title: Date With Nature
programme.desc: Slip away to Thailand's secluded shores and hidden streams, where aquatic life glides through crystal-clear waters.

programme.title: Coral Kingdom
programme.desc: Majestic arrangements of colorful coral sway to the rhythm of the tide, with plenty of aquatic visitors stopping by to enjoy the scenery.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S1 - "Rome"
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace heads to the Italian Capital, Rome, for a Weekend Break. Over the course of 3 days he eats amazing local dishes, tours the sights – both by foot and by scooter - and even tries his hand as a Gladiator.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S1 - "Barcelona"
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace heads to Barcelona for a Weekend Break. Over the course of 3 days he tries incredible local food and drink and gets involved with local customs in a city where there always seems to be a party to join!

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Cape Verde"
programme.desc: An expat fisherman scores a big catch in Cape Verde, while another struggles in Spain. A French B&B receives its first review, and a boutique hotel launches a wedding business.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Boutique Wedding"
programme.desc: Steve and his mother-in-law serve food at their Spanish bar. Ray and Yvonne host a wedding at their hotel. Two ex-actors try to bring drama to Bordeaux amid a new baby’s arrival.

programme.title: Breaking Beyond S1 - "Goma, Congo"
programme.desc: Bboy Junior is going back where he was born, The Democratic Republic of Congo. World champion Shigekix will join him, visiting Africa for the first time. Together they will explore the dance culture in the city of Goma.

programme.title: Roads Unknown India S1 - "The Unforgettable Journey"
programme.desc: Jess explores the ancient towns and iconic landmarks of Rajasthan. The crew continues to Jaipur, uncovering more cultural treasures and reunites with Pulak for the final stretch to Delhi. After a few bike troubles, Jess and her team arrive in Delhi, reflecting on the unforgettable journey they've shared together.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "Open Concept (Part 2)"
programme.desc: Melinda surprises Dan with some new information, but can Steve surprise them both by showing them the perfect property?

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "A Big Investment (Part 1)"
programme.desc: Vancouver-based couple Jim and Shannon are looking to relocate to their hometown of Toronto with their two daughters. With a budget of 10 million dollars, they enlist the help of savvy realtor Sofie Kehdi.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "A Big Investment (Part 2)"
programme.desc: While it might first seem that Jim and Shannon are on the same page, the house hunt proves that they have some differing opinions on what makes a good investment. Sofie will have her hands full trying to meet the demands of her conflicted clients!

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S1 - "The Kimberly, Istanbul"
programme.desc: Scotty visits the Valley of the Mist in Coffs Harbor then peddles his way in the Victorian high country and discovers some indigenous art in the Kimberley while Alana visits the famous spice markets in Istanbul.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S1 - "Topkapi Palace"
programme.desc: Scotty walks to the Mitchell Falls in the Kimberley while Alana continues her trip in Turkey visiting Topkapi Palace and takes a relaxing cruise. Skye takes a culinary look in one of Australia’s top fresh food markets Adelaide Central market.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S1 - "Bungle Bungle National Park"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Bungle Bungle National Park
programme.desc: Scotty heads to one of Australia’s iconic destinations the Bungle Bungle National Park while Alana prepares a special dish with chef David Farmer in Adelaide and Alana ticks off one of her bucket list destinations - Galipoli.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Chiang Mai - Eze"
programme.desc: From Asia to Europe this episode explore the cultures and magnificent architecture in Chiang Mai and Eze in the South of France.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Ice Hotel, Sweden - Bangkok"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Ice Hotel, Sweden - Bangkok
programme.desc: From the hustle and bustle of Bangkok to the freezing temperatures of the Ice Hotel in Sweden, this episode is all about polar opposites, fire and ice.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "St Tropez - St Lucia"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 St Tropez - St Lucia
programme.desc: Romantic Caribbean Islands and sleeping fishing villages turned European jet set resorts, this episode travels from St Lucia to St Tropez.

programme.title: Big Little Footprints S1 - "Travel That Transforms Fiji"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E07 Travel That Transforms Fiji
programme.desc: We travel to an idyllic island in the South Pacific, known for its enviable coral reef and chance to swim with giant Manta Rays! We also visit the local village to learn about its important relationship with the resort and hear how tourism has helped

programme.title: Big Little Footprints S1 - "Australia's Gold Coast Famous for Fun"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 Australia's Gold Coast Famous for Fun
programme.desc: In this episode, we travel to Australia’s favorite Playground - the Gold Coast, to prove family vacations can have it all! Fun and education; beach and the rainforest; roller coasters and wildlife. It’s a destination that’s as beautiful as it is FUN!

programme.title: Big Little Footprints S1 - "Iconic Downunder - Lady Elliot Island"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 Iconic Downunder - Lady Elliot Island
programme.desc: From cute Koalas to the world heritage Great Barrier Reef, this family ticks off seeing two of Australia’s biggest icons and learning about their vulnerability and how as tourists we can help.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S1 - "Berlin"
programme.desc: Gregg heads to the German capital for a 3 day trip. He gets to the heart of Berlin’s food scene and gets involved in the beer culture. Between meals he explores the city’s often turbulent past and indulges his love of the musical Cabaret!

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S1 - "Amsterdam"
programme.desc: Gregg heads to the Dutch capital for a long weekend where he tries out amazing food and drink, gets involved with fascinating local customs, explores life on the canals and uncovers the historical reasons Amsterdam has such a relaxed attitude.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S1 - "Vienna"
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace heads to the home of the Christmas market, Vienna, to get to the bottom of what makes them so special, checking out great festive food, fascinating traditions and warming drinks!

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Fisherman's Dream"
programme.desc: Bed & breakfast owners receive feedback from their final guests of the season. Will weddings make or break a Spanish B&B, and can the Costa’s newest fisherman make his dream a reality?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Dive into Marbella"
programme.desc: Can a family from North Yorkshire run a business in the middle of Europe’s only desert? A car enthusiast pushes to sell spares in France. Two expats launch a dive centre in Marbella.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Mediterranean Scuba"
programme.desc: A British family faces local resistance while opening a business in the Spanish desert. Scuba divers search for a century-old wreck. A French townhouse begins its transformation.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "Magical Home (Part 1)"
programme.desc: Off on another adventure! This episode takes Mike Donia on a whirlwind tour of Southeast Asia as he searches for a home for his clients, Antonio and Kim.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "Magical Home (Part 2)"
programme.desc: Featuring properties in Thailand, Indonesia, and the Philippines, each country has its own unique charms to offer the would-be buyers. Monkeys, mangoes and mansions are in store for Mike as he continues his hunt for that perfect property.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "Income for a Rainy Day (Part 1)"
programme.sub-title: S02 : E21 Income for a Rainy Day (Part 1)
programme.desc: Vezi and Deborah love the house they purchased last season in Stouffville, but they are ready to make the move to the big city. They enlist the help of Inez Kudryk to show them the best properties she can find in Toronto.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S1 - "National Wine Centre"
programme.desc: Scotty’s Kimberley adventure comes to an end and Skye hits the National Wine Centre in South Australia while Alana says good bye to Istanbul.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Thailand, China"
programme.desc: Join Scotty as he embarks on a trip through Thailand, before visiting QLD…and Alana arrives in China.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "China’s Great Wall"
programme.desc: Scotty’s trip to Thailand heats up at a local cooking school, before he takes a segue tour of Brisbane, and Alana visits one of the wonders of the world – China’s Great Wall.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "MONACO & VENICE"
programme.desc: Monaco is the home of extravagance, but then let’s not forget the legendary Hotel Cipriani in the wonderful city Venice that is also featured in this glitzy luxury extravaganza.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "SUPERYACHTS"
programme.desc: This glitzy adventure starts in the idyllic town Portofino -Italy, then our super-yacht moors offshore Monaco where we board a privately owned 212 ft, 80 million euro super yacht – The White Rose of Drachs.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "SWITZERLAND & SWEDEN"
programme.desc: Zermatt is one of the most exclusive Ski Resorts in the world. In Sweden, we stayed in award winning 'Victory Hotel' a welcoming and exclusive hotel with a personal atmosphere.

programme.title: Australia's Amazing Homes S1 - "Little Gems"
programme.desc: Modern city living has it challenges mostly around space. Johanna and Pete look at homes that make the most out of the space they have. These little gems may change the way you think about living in small spaces.

programme.title: Australia's Amazing Homes S1 - "Incredible Conversions"
programme.desc: Converting old buildings like warehouses and churches into modern homes is not for the faint hearted. Discover some truly amazing conversions that manage to successfully combine modern design without elminating the original charm of the old buildings - and the results are spectacular.

programme.title: Rhys Darby in Japan S1 - "The Masters"
programme.desc: Rhys learns what makes Japanese audiences tick from a pair of comedy masters, and he puts on a comedy show of his own. Also, New Zealand's mascot Pabu-chan is finally revealed to the world.

programme.title: Rhys Darby in Japan S1 - "The Rise of Pabu-chan"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 The Rise of Pabu-chan
programme.desc: The popularity of New Zealand's mascot, Pabu-chan, grows at the Sapporo Snow Festival, and then he gets a chance to hit the big time. Rhys also travels to the Okinawa Islands in search of the beautiful and fleeting cherry blossoms.

programme.title: The Delinquent Gourmet S1 - "Jamaica"
programme.desc: Gord Martin is in Jamaica to down a little food and raise a little hell. First he heads to Hellshire Bay, then on to the Blue Mountains to learn authentic lip smackin' Jamaican cuisine from a renowned local chef... But mastering those dumplings is harder than it looks!

programme.title: The Delinquent Gourmet S1 - "Mexico"
programme.desc: Gord Martin is in Mexico where he dives off the tourist map to reveal the authentic cuisine of the Mayan Riviera. After stopping in at a well-stocked tequileria for an extensive tasting session, he samples a street vendor's colourful taco varieties, and visits a supermarket with a vast array of local and exotic produce.

programme.title: Khanh Ong's Wild Food S1 - "Mornington Peninsula, VIC"
programme.desc: Chef Khanh Ong sets up camp on the magnificent Mornington Peninsula, Victoria to cook a three Course meal inspired by his travels through the series. Each meal being a nod to some of the wild and wonderful locations that he has travelled to.

programme.title: Capturing Winter S1 - "Tristan 'Shu' Lebeschu"
programme.desc: Renowned winter photographer, Tristan Shu, attempts his dream project; a photograph that combines astrophotography with action sports. His aim is to capture ski and snowboard tricks at night, set against the Milky-way and the spectacular Mont-Blanc.

programme.title: Capturing Winter S1 - "Aurélie Gonin"
programme.desc: Aurélie Gonin is one of the few women working in winter action sports filmmaking. Over the years she gathered expertise in every aspect of her field. We join as she directs an elite ski and snowboarding qualification event of the Freeride World Tour.

programme.title: Food Unknown S1 - "Mexico"
programme.desc: Jonathan Legg is from California and he thinks he knows Mexican cuisine. This notion gets shattered as he experiences the onslaught of food in Mexico city! Between trying scorpion tacos and dodging shady markets in San Juan, he learns the dark art of sacred drinks, like pulque and mezcal, now turned into social libations. His journey also takes him to Oaxaca where he gets the low down on the fabled Mole sauce, and a long haul to a remote village reveals a secret ceremony that has origins of dark arts!

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Cape Breton Island, Canada"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Cape Breton Island, Canada
programme.desc: By pick-up truck, the brothers route is the culturally and geographically rich island of Cape Breton and the Cabot Trail. Lobster fishing, hooking, moose sightings, Bingo, Scottish ceilidhs, Mi'kmaq drum session, it's all happening in this episode.

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Volcan Madera, Nicaragua"
programme.desc: The brothers attain their goal of riding motorcycles to the heart of Nicaragua, through the jungle to the isolated beaches of the Pacific coast and back inland: through Spanish-colonial towns, local villages and the huge volcanic island of Ometepe.

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Yorkshire Dales, England"
programme.desc: Imagine a 26-pub, 7-day, 60-mile pub-crawl! Doing a traditional ‘ramble’ through the stunning Yorkshire Dales region of northern England, the brother’s stride through the moors and dales and peaks and valleys - legs and livers intact.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Croatia"
programme.desc: Max & Benj Cave travel to Croatia to meet up with local parkour experts and undertake an epic run in an abandoned aluminium factory.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Santorini"
programme.desc: A challenge to race each other and a labyrinth of narrow streets and hidden passages to navigate will make this one of the trickiest challenges.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Best of ep. 1 to 5"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Best of ep. 1 to 5
programme.desc: Parkour athletes Max & Benj Cave look back over their travels so far and discuss the best places for practising parkour.

programme.title: Piri’s Tiki Tour S6 - "Tūranga-nui-a-Kiwa"
programme.desc: Piri and ex Crusader Tu Umaga-Marshall are in Tūranga-nui-a-Kiwa (Gisborne), diving for Haku (Kingfish), boxing with the Patu Tahi Boxing Club and meeting Gisborne’s local horse whisperer Sam Sidney.

programme.title: Piri’s Tiki Tour S6 - "Te Urewera"
programme.desc: Piri and ex Warrior Wairangi Koopu are deep in Te Urewera. Hosted by Waimana’s local all female hunting group the “Wahine Slayaz”, they hunt Deer, gather Oysters and make Rongoā with Koro Hori Hillman.

programme.title: Piri’s Tiki Tour S6 - "Taupō"
programme.desc: Piri and ex Crusader Tu Umaga-Marshall are in Taupō, on the lake targeting Trout, having a run around with the Taupō Juniors Touch Team and learning some Taupō Hītori from Piri’s high school mate Awhina Eru.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "Marriage Lows"
programme.desc: Jason experiences insecurity about challenges that the couple are, quite literally, dropped into. From falling out of the sky to a rappel into a dark cave, will the couple be able to work together or will they end up stranded in the dark?

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "You Do What?"
programme.desc: The couple head to south western Oklahoma to climb at Quartz Mountain, but when Jason's confidence is shaken during a failed slab climb, Tayler realizes she may need to prepare to carry a larger load on their journey out west.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "Know Pain, Know Gain"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Know Pain, Know Gain
programme.desc: Failed rock climbing, failed training...struggling marriage? When everything seems to have gone wrong on their road trip in Oklahoma, Tayler and Jason are faced with the question..."are we biting off more than we can chew?" as they head to Wyoming.

programme.title: Epic Exploring S2 - "Charleroi"
programme.desc: Local exploring in industrial Charleroi, brings Cody to abandoned factories and an unknown spot full of colorful graffiti. An old casino awaits to be explored.

programme.title: Epic Exploring S2 - "Nicosia"
programme.desc: Josh is in Cyprus for a local exploring adventure across the island on a roadtrip from Nicosia to an abandoned amusement park, an asbestos mine and a shipwreck.

programme.title: Epic Exploring S2 - "New Orleans"
programme.desc: Cody is with Lexi Limitless in the ‘Big Easy’ to explore New Orleans, visiting an abandoned Charity Hospital before meeting Brutus, the giant alligator.

programme.title: The Armchair Adventurer S1 - "The Animals of Oudtshoorn"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E22 The Animals of Oudtshoorn
programme.desc: Xander starts his day by receiving a surprising phone call from Adam before heading to Chandelier Game Lodge to visit Africa’s most exquisite animals.

programme.title: The Armchair Adventurer S1 - "Creations of Prins Albert"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E23 Creations of Prins Albert
programme.desc: Despite unforeseen travel complications, Xander travels through a picturesque mountain pass to Prince Albert to enjoy the olive farms and other specialties the town has to offer.

programme.title: The Armchair Adventurer S1 - "The Ghosts of Matjiesfontein"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E24 The Ghosts of Matjiesfontein
programme.desc: With one final trip ahead of them, Xander and his crew make their way from the scorching desert of Lindbergh to the haunted ghost town of Matjiesfontein.

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Erg Chigagga, Sahara, Morocco"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 Erg Chigagga, Sahara, Morocco
programme.desc: From Marrakesh, the brothers take a series of local Berber taxis over the High Atlas Mountains and down into the Draa Valley until the road ends at the edge of the great sand sea of the Sahara where they bury some treasure.

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Arviat, Nunavut, Canada"
programme.desc: Heading to the far north into the Arctic to learn about the Inuit’s traditional culture and incredible way of life, the brothers accompany an elder and young hunter on a 200-mile skidoo hunt across the barren land and frozen sea ice.

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Kushtia, Bangladesh"
programme.desc: The brothers traverse the country’s interconnecting network of waterways by ferryboat. From shrimp baron, to peasant fishermen, lofty temple ruins to anonymous chai wallahs, they make their way to Kushtia, home to the shrine of Guru Lalone Shaw.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Cornwall"
programme.desc: Francesca Chiorando dives into life in Cornwall to discover the ancient practices of witchcraft and smuggling.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Iceland"
programme.desc: Francesca is on a quest to discover why Icelandic people believe in mythical creatures. Is it folk law or are there really trolls lurking in the wilderness?

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Snowdonia"
programme.desc: Francesca Chiorando goes on a high-octane, nail-biting adventure in Snowdonia, one of the remotest corners of the UK.

programme.title: Piri’s Tiki Tour S6 - "Whakatū"
programme.desc: Piri and ex Warrior Wairangi Koopu are down in Whakatū (Nelson). They’re hosted by Cyril Collier targeting Snapper, put to the test by Raniera Petersen with a Mau Rakau lesson and visit the Anatoki Salmon Farm.

programme.title: Piri’s Tiki Tour S6 - "Te Matau-a-Māui"
programme.desc: Piri and ex Māori All Black Shannon Paku are in Te Matau-a-Māui (Hawkes Bay). They’re hosted by Rob Beard of Beard Brothers Sausages, targeting Koura and Gurnard. They also visit Piri’s old stomping grounds of Te Aute College with ex-classmate Tautoko Ratu.

programme.title: Piri’s Tiki Tour S6 - "Whakatāne"
programme.desc: Piri and ex Crusader Tu Umaga-Marshall are in Whakatane. Hosted by Henare Ruru and Henare Rangitaawa of The Dive Squad, they target Haku (Kingfish), give tips and tricks to the Taneatua and Paroa Rugby Clubs and go on a Boar hunt with Troy Petersen.

programme.title: Breaking Beyond S1 - "Sao Paulo, Brazil"
programme.desc: Russian Bgirl Kastet is visiting Sao Paulo, the home of Bboy Neguin. Together they will explore the dance, breaking and hip hop culture in the streets of Sao Paulo. Meeting up with legends and learning Capoeira, Jiu Jitsu and Passinho.

programme.title: Breaking Beyond S1 - "Las Vegas, USA"
programme.desc: Brazilian Bboy Neguin is visiting his friend Bboy Ronnie in his hometown Las Vegas. Ronnie istaking Neguin to meet all the different artists and dancers who are able to make a living inVegas by doing what they love most: performing on stage.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "The Boss"
programme.desc: An expat adjusts to working with her husband after 34 years of teaching. A scuba business considers relocating to the UK. Bar owners in Spain's desert face their busiest night.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Spanish Boot Camp"
programme.desc: A newcomer launches a beach bootcamp in Spain. Vineyard owners brace for a critical season and a French townhouse renovation reaches a milestone.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S2 - "Budapest"
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace spends 3 days in Budapest, uncovering its invasion-shaped food culture and exploring the city on foot and by Cold War jeep.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S2 - "Edinburgh"
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace explores Edinburgh over 3 days, diving into its history, tasting local classics, and braving a dip in the Firth of Forth.

programme.title: DownieLive Travels By Train S1 - "Manitoba"
programme.desc: Michael eats and explores his way through Winnipeg, tries curling, and takes a road trip that ends at a relaxing outdoor spa.

programme.title: DownieLive Travels By Train S1 - "Saskatchewan"
programme.desc: Michael visits a farm in Saskatchewan, celebrates his birthday on a road trip, trains with a football team, and stays at a retreat.

programme.title: City Beats S1 - "Paris"
programme.desc: DJ and house music producer Carly Foxx travels to Paris, the city of love, for inspiration and discovers its diversity and a creative trend called retrofuture.

programme.title: City Beats S1 - "Berlin"
programme.desc: DJ and house music producer Carly Foxx journeys Berlin, one of the worlds undisputed techno capitals, and discovers the sound of freedom buried in its history.

programme.title: City Beats S1 - "Ibiza"
programme.desc: DJ-producer Carly Foxx travels to the clubbing capital of the world to inspire her music track and discovers Ibiza’s alternative spiritual side.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "North Carolina"
programme.desc: Meet Lisa, a former lawyer who escaped the rat race and moved into a campervan after the early death of her mother. Lisa embarks on a cross-country road trip, peeking inside homes on wheels, and connecting with the incredible community of nomads.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Mississippi, Alabama & Arkansas"
programme.desc: A hurricane in the south forces Lisa to change direction. We meet southern Van Lifers living under the radar in a state where the lifestyle is judged harshly. Tragically, Lisa’s story takes a heavy turn when she discovers that her father has died.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Texas"
programme.desc: A colourful graffiti artist in Austin shares her story of loss with Lisa and gives her a tarot reading. We travel to rural Texas to overnight with Jupiter: an outspoken, non-binary person of colour, living in their vintage RV while studying law.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "New York"
programme.desc: Yann Horowitz delves into NYC's skate scene, visiting shops, meeting legends, and exploring iconic spots. He highlights skateboarding's community and inclusivity, expressing affection for the city and the connections forged through his passion.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "Brooklyn"
programme.desc: Yann Horowitz skateboards with the KCDC skateboarding community in Brooklyn. Where he explores gaming, art and grilling with Crossy, Natalie, Jacob, Eddie and Nate, culminating in a joyful skate event showcasing community, inclusivity and fun.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "New Jersey"
programme.desc: Yann delves into New Jersey's skateboarding culture, meeting skateboarders who are into, fashion, horror, comedy, tattooing, and rug-making. He explores NJ Skate Shop and Freedom Skate Park, encountering Fred Gall and Brian Anderson.

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "Cape Town"
programme.desc: In Cape Town, the journey unfolds, connecting with mentors, experiencing creative breakthroughs, spiritual rituals, and ancestral connections. From this transformative experience, Marilia discovers the power of inner strength and connection.

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "Cornwall"
programme.desc: Marilia explores Cornwall, visits Bodmin Moor, meets a life coach and artist. She engages in nature activities, learns animal massage, forages seaweed, and relaxes at a spa. The episode captures Cornwall's landscapes and immersive experiences.

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "St. Pete"
programme.desc: Marilia spreads love on her Treasure Island journey, capturing hidden gems from tranquil beaches to transformative sound healing. Meeting artists, experiencing Dolphin Yoga, participating in a flamenco dance class, and sharing a tea ceremony.

programme.title: Blindspots S1 - "Los Angeles"
programme.desc: James returns to his former home city of Los Angeles, where he reconnects with old friends working in accessibility in the entertainment industry.

programme.title: Blindspots S1 - "Seattle / Vancouver"
programme.desc: After a surprese invite to visit the head of accessibility at Microsoft's headquarters in Seattle, James heads to Vancouver, Canada's jewel of the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Blindspots S1 - "New York City"
programme.desc: How does the bustling island of steel and stone fare for those with disabilities? James sets out to discover what it's like to travel blind in the Big Apple.

programme.title: City Beats S1 - "Europe"
programme.desc: DJ and house music producer Carly Foxx looks back at her journey to the music dance capitals of Europe to inspire her final track.

programme.title: Breaking Beyond S1 - "Tokyo, Japan"
programme.desc: Vegas Bboy Ronnie is traveling to Japan to visit Bboy Shigekix in his hometown Tokyo. Shigekix will take Ronnie on an exciting Journey to show the Japanese culture and it's unique style of breaking and hip-hop.

programme.title: Breaking Beyond S1 - "Rotterdam, The Netherlands"
programme.desc: In the city of Rotterdam, three times world champion, Menno, hosts b-boy Junior from France. Both are considered legends. Menno takes Junior on a trip he will never forget and meet people who have all left their mark in the Dutch breaking community.

programme.title: The Streets S1 - "Detroit"
programme.desc: Punk rocker Jon-Mikal formed his band The Idiot Kids as a way to deal with his sexuality. After coming out to his family at 21, he uses writing and his stage performance as a form of therapy.

programme.title: DownieLive Travels By Train S1 - "Alberta"
programme.desc: Michael visits an Alberta ranch, sees the snowy Rockies, stays at a rail hotel, and visits the Badlands and a giant mall.

programme.title: DownieLive Travels By Train S1 - "British Columbia"
programme.desc: Michael visits BC for a canoe tour, joins a rescue team on a hovercraft, plays pond hockey, and takes his final train trip.

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "Joshua Tree"
programme.desc: In Joshua Tree, Gabi shared astrology insights, Alchemy provided sound healing, and Marilia discovered spiritual connection. Guide Andrew introduced mindfulness, Doug showcased grounding through sprouting, concluding with reflection in hot springs.

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "LA"
programme.desc: In LA, we hike with wolfs, Ayurveda insights with Jay and a delicious meal from Karla. Marilia takes an adventurous leap from an airplane, showing the freedom in extreme sports.Tracy showcases the joy of pursuing passions with WCMX at the skate park.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "Philadelphia"
programme.desc: Yann immerses himself in Philadelphia's skateboarding culture. He explores Nocturnal Skate Shop eats a Philly cheesesteak, visits Cloud 9 pottery studio, admires Adam's art collection, skates with Celina, and experiences the creativity of FDR.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "St. Petersburg"
programme.desc: Yann explores St. Petersburg's skateboarding community, visiting Anchor Skate shop and meeting Jay Turner, Kris Markovitch, Jake White, Naomi Glueck, Evan Starkey, and Alex Charos. He highlights creativity, self-expression, and the vibrant community.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "Phoenix"
programme.desc: Yann delves into Phoenix's diverse skateboarding scene, exploring Cowtown Skateboards. Meeting Hendren a sfashion designer, Christians band, Garnak, Triple paint designs, Justin a blacksmith and Douglas Miles Jr the owner of Apache skateboards.

programme.title: Blindspots S1 - "London"
programme.desc: James heads across the pond to discover what life for a blind traveler is like in a world capital like London.

programme.title: Blindspots S1 - "United Kingdom"
programme.desc: Escaping the hustle and bustle of the big city, James takes the train to visit the rest of England - proving the rest of England is more accessible than one might think.

programme.title: Capturing Winter S1 - "Martin Bochatay and Arthur Maneint"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Martin Bochatay and Arthur Maneint
programme.desc: Martin and Arthur are two of the world’s best FPV drone pilots. Now the duo team up with pro skier and Olympic medalist, Kevin Rolland, for a unique project ; mount a cinema camera on a racing drone and attempt to film some never-before-seen action.

programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.2
programme.desc: This five-episode series explores China's growing cultural confidence, showing how a revival of traditional culture is fueling "chain reactions" that are reshaping Chinese manufacturing and development. In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.2
programme.desc: This five-episode series explores China's growing cultural confidence, showing how a revival of traditional culture is fueling "chain reactions" that are reshaping Chinese manufacturing and development. In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.2

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: Seafaring Tales: Fu Ships
programme.desc: In past centuries, transporting goods along the Maritime Silk Road could be a hazardous undertaking. Ships often faced fierce storms which blew up on the high seas. To reduce the danger, China’s shipbuilders came up with a solution. They developed Fu ships, commonly known as junks, incorporating a special watertight bulkhead technology. Their revolutionary design allowed the ships to withstand the highest winds and roughest waters.The Maritime Silk Road was an ancient trading route that connected China to Southeast and South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. As a key passageway for trade and cultural exchange, it thrived for well over a thousand years, bringing Chinese tea, silk,...

programme.title: Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe
programme.desc: On the occasion of the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between China and the EU, Inheritors of China launches a special multimedia series titled “Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe.” Taking culture as the starting point, the series responds to public discourse by telling the real-life stories of Europeans living in China. Through their perspectives, it vividly illustrates how tradition and modernity spark mutual inspiration between China and Europe—showcasing a relationship of shared growth, empowerment, and enduring partnership.The series travels through cities including Beijing, Shanghai, Jinan, and Datong, focusing on Europeans of different ages and...

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: Life as a Miao Embroidery Girl
programme.desc: In this episode, host Eva May travels to Maomaohe Village in Leishan County, Guizhou Province — a place renowned for its exquisite Miao embroidery. She spends a week immersing herself in the daily lives of local embroidery artisans, learning traditional Miao folk songs, and rising to the challenge of creating a piece of Miaoxiu embroidery with special cultural significance.

programme.title: Life as a Miao Embroidery Girl
programme.desc: In this episode, host Eva May travels to Maomaohe Village in Leishan County, Guizhou Province — a place renowned for its exquisite Miao embroidery. She spends a week immersing herself in the daily lives of local embroidery artisans, learning traditional Miao folk songs, and rising to the challenge of creating a piece of Miaoxiu embroidery with special cultural significance.

programme.title: Life as a Miao Silversmith
programme.desc: In this episode, Eva May steps into Maliao Village — a century-old silversmith village nestled in Leishan, Guizhou Province. Over the course of a week, she dives into the craft and daily lives of Miao men who carry on the silversmithing legacy. She works side by side with local silversmiths day in and day out, delving into the culture and techniques behind Miao silverware. She even joins master silversmiths for a live-stream event, before engaging in discussions with university professors about how traditional craftsmanship can fuel rural revitalization. On top of that, she witnesses the breathtaking sight of "maidens adorned in silver and flowers" at the grand Miao New Year celebrations,...

programme.title: New Year with the Miao
programme.desc: In this episode, it’s Miao New Year, and that means multitudes of merrymaking! Fishing, drinking, bullfighting… Join the host Min-Zhui Lee, among the magnificent mountains of Leishan, where it’s all happening for the ethnic Miao.

programme.title: New Year with the Miao

programme.title: Valentine’s for the Pumi People
programme.desc: In this episode, we record the grand celebration of the traditional Pumi Valentine's Day in Luoguqing, Lanping County, Yunnan Province. Every year, on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, young men and women from the local community gather on the vast meadows of Luoguqing to take part in singing and dancing activities in pursuit of marriage. During the program, the host interacts with local people of the Pumi and other ethnic groups, showcasing the traditional plateau lifestyle of the Pumi people, their distinctive firepit culture, as well as the community’s brand-new look in the new era. All these scenes unfold against the lively backdrop of the Valentine’s Day activities, fostering a...

programme.title: Valentine's with the Snake King
programme.desc: Every year on Qixi Festival (Chinese Valentine's Day), the town of Zhanghu in Nanping, Fujian Province hosts a folk custom event: the Snake King Parade. Local residents carry the statue of the Snake King from the temple, along with specially prepared pythons, and parade through the streets and alleys. Wherever the procession passes, residents and shopkeepers greet them with firecrackers and burn incense to pray, hoping to receive the unique blessing of the Snake King. This distinctive folk activity is rarely seen elsewhere across China. Taking this event as its highlight, the episode leads audiences on a deep dive into the towns and villages of northern Fujian, delivering a highly...

On top of that, she witnesses the breathtaking sight of "maidens adorned in silver and flowers" at the grand Miao New Year celebrations,...

programme.title: Life as a Miao Silversmith
programme.desc: In this episode, Eva May steps into Maliao Village — a century-old silversmith village nestled in Leishan, Guizhou Province. programme.title: New Year with the Miao
programme.desc: In this episode, it’s Miao New Year, and that means multitudes of merrymaking! Fishing, drinking, bullfighting… Join the host Min-Zhui Lee, among the magnificent mountains of Leishan, where it’s all happening for the ethnic Miao.

programme.title: Valentine’s for the Pumi People
programme.desc: In this episode, we record the grand celebration of the traditional Pumi Valentine's Day in Luoguqing, Lanping County, Yunnan Province. Every year, on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, young men and women from the local community gather on the vast meadows of Luoguqing to take part in singing and dancing activities in pursuit of marriage. During the program, the host interacts with local people of the Pumi and other ethnic groups, showcasing the traditional plateau lifestyle of the Pumi people, their distinctive firepit culture, as well as the community’s brand-new look in the new era. All these scenes unfold against the lively backdrop of the Valentine’s Day activities, fostering a...

programme.title: Valentine's with the Snake King
programme.desc: Every year on Qixi Festival (Chinese Valentine's Day), the town of Zhanghu in Nanping, Fujian Province hosts a folk custom event: the Snake King Parade. Local residents carry the statue of the Snake King from the temple, along with specially prepared pythons, and parade through the streets and alleys. Wherever the procession passes, residents and shopkeepers greet them with firecrackers and burn incense to pray, hoping to receive the unique blessing of the Snake King. This distinctive folk activity is rarely seen elsewhere across China. Taking this event as its highlight, the episode leads audiences on a deep dive into the towns and villages of northern Fujian, delivering a highly...

programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.2
programme.desc: This five-episode series explores China's growing cultural confidence, showing how a revival of traditional culture is fueling "chain reactions" that are reshaping Chinese manufacturing and development. In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: Seafaring Tales: Fu Ships
programme.desc: In past centuries, transporting goods along the Maritime Silk Road could be a hazardous undertaking. Ships often faced fierce storms which blew up on the high seas. To reduce the danger, China’s shipbuilders came up with a solution. They developed Fu ships, commonly known as junks, incorporating a special watertight bulkhead technology. Their revolutionary design allowed the ships to withstand the highest winds and roughest waters.The Maritime Silk Road was an ancient trading route that connected China to Southeast and South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. As a key passageway for trade and cultural exchange, it thrived for well over a thousand years, bringing Chinese tea, silk,...

programme.title: Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe
programme.desc: On the occasion of the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between China and the EU, Inheritors of China launches a special multimedia series titled “Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe.” Taking culture as the starting point, the series responds to public discourse by telling the real-life stories of Europeans living in China. Through their perspectives, it vividly illustrates how tradition and modernity spark mutual inspiration between China and Europe—showcasing a relationship of shared growth, empowerment, and enduring partnership.The series travels through cities including Beijing, Shanghai, Jinan, and Datong, focusing on Europeans of different ages and...

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: An Encounter with China’s Endangered Monkeys
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve in Diqing Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Yunnan Province, to learn about and participate in the conservation work for the rare and precious national treasure: the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. The presenter ventures into the forest, experiences the daily life of local forest rangers, and witnesses the harmonious relationship between humans and monkeys. We interview "the Monkey Father"—Long Yongcheng, chief scientist of The Nature Conservancy (TNC) and leader of China's Primate Expert Group—who shares stories about the two decades of efforts dedicated to protecting the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. We also go to the mountains to...

programme.title: Celadon Ou Music
programme.desc: Celadon ou music is also called "bowl music.” The reason is that the ancient Chinese word for bowl sounds like ou. The music originated simply as improvised rhythms tapped out on bowls. It was this spontaneity that made celadon ou so popular. Celadon ou music was at the height of its popularity over 1,100 years ago. But with time, it faded from history. At one point, it was even on the verge of being lost forever. After hundreds of years of obscurity, it re-emerged at the end of last century.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.”

programme.title: The Sichuan Zhuqin
programme.desc: Sichuan Zhuqin is a national intangible cultural heritage in China. Nowadays, its performances are constantly innovated, with new styles such as ensemble emerging. Creators have broken traditional performance methods, integrating Zhuqin with traditional Chinese instruments like the guzheng and erhu, and also experimenting with contemporary music forms such as rap and folk rock, aiming to make this traditional art appeal to more young audiences.

programme.title: A Lifelong Journey of Xuan Paper
programme.desc: The host travels to Jingxian, Anhui—known as the hometown of Xuan paper—to experience the intricate process of making this traditional paper. Along the way, they uncover the secrets behind its thousand-year legacy and witness the craftsmanship of Xuan paper inheritors at every stage. Through vivid, personal stories, the episode reveals the hidden struggles and quiet perseverance that have kept this intangible cultural heritage alive across generations.

Creators have broken traditional performance methods, integrating Zhuqin with traditional Chinese instruments like the guzheng and erhu, and also experimenting with contemporary music forms such as rap and folk rock, aiming to make this traditional art appeal to more young audiences.

programme.title: A Lifelong Journey of Xuan Paper
programme.desc: The host travels to Jingxian, Anhui—known as the hometown of Xuan paper—to experience the intricate process of making this traditional paper. Along the way, they uncover the secrets behind its thousand-year legacy and witness the craftsmanship of Xuan paper inheritors at every stage. Through vivid, personal stories, the episode reveals the hidden struggles and quiet perseverance that have kept this intangible cultural heritage alive across generations.

programme.title: An Encounter with China’s Endangered Monkeys
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve in Diqing Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Yunnan Province, to learn about and participate in the conservation work for the rare and precious national treasure: the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. The presenter ventures into the forest, experiences the daily life of local forest rangers, and witnesses the harmonious relationship between humans and monkeys. We interview "the Monkey Father"—Long Yongcheng, chief scientist of The Nature Conservancy (TNC) and leader of China's Primate Expert Group—who shares stories about the two decades of efforts dedicated to protecting the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. We also go to the mountains to...

programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.3
programme.desc: This five-episode series explores China's growing cultural confidence, showing how a revival of traditional culture is fueling "chain reactions" that are reshaping Chinese manufacturing and development. In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.title: Celadon Ou Music
programme.desc: Celadon ou music is also called "bowl music.” The reason is that the ancient Chinese word for bowl sounds like ou. The music originated simply as improvised rhythms tapped out on bowls. It was this spontaneity that made celadon ou so popular. Celadon ou music was at the height of its popularity over 1,100 years ago. But with time, it faded from history. At one point, it was even on the verge of being lost forever. After hundreds of years of obscurity, it re-emerged at the end of last century.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.”

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.1
programme.desc: The Xiangjiang River runs 800 kilometers through the whole of Hunan Province. Hailed as the mother river of Hunan, it serves as a vital waterway connecting the Yangtze and Pearl River systems. Its source is located in Yongzhou, a land of abundant water resources, rich natural treasures and outstanding people. In this episode, we focus on three young women from Yongzhou: Pan Haiying, a dance teacher; Huang Cuilan, an inheritor of brocade weaving; and Hu Xin, a custodian of Nüshu culture. We document snippets of their daily lives and work, to reflect the profound cultural heritage along the Xiangjiang River and the value pursuits of the women living on its banks.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, to meet more outstanding women from the cities of Hengyang and Zhuzhou. By documenting snippets of the daily lives and work of a paper-cutting artist, the director of the Hengyang Huagu Opera Troupe, and an inheritor of Liling porcelain craftsmanship, we gain vivid insights into the distinctive cultural traditions of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, traveling to the cities of Zhuzhou and Xiangtan to meet another group of extraordinary women. By capturing snippets of the lives and work of an electric locomotive designer, a flight instructor, a painter, and a poverty alleviation volunteer, we showcase the unique cultural and humanistic charm of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Changsha — the economic and cultural hub of Hunan Province, and a core city in the Yangtze River Middle Reaches Economic Zone. Our lens turns to the "Xiangjiang Ladies" who work in Changsha’s high-tech and equipment manufacturing sectors. Their daily lives and professional endeavors reflect the city’s innovative vitality, while their after-work gatherings also showcase the dazzling night views along both banks of the Xiangjiang River, highlighting Changsha’s distinct charm as a vibrant capital of leisure and entertainment.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.desc: On the occasion of the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between China and the EU, Inheritors of China launches a special multimedia series titled “Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe.” Taking culture as the starting point, the series responds to public discourse by telling the real-life stories of Europeans living in China. Through their perspectives, it vividly illustrates how tradition and modernity spark mutual inspiration between China and Europe—showcasing a relationship of shared growth, empowerment, and enduring partnership.The series travels through cities including Beijing, Shanghai, Jinan, and Datong, focusing on Europeans of different ages and...

programme.title: The sound of casting
programme.desc: For centuries, the fires of craftsmanship have never faded in Pingyao. In this UNESCO World Heritage city once famed for trade, three ancient metalworking traditions — sword casting, gold and silver crafting, and iron forging — continue to thrive. This episode steps into the workshops where artisans shape metal with fire and devotion. Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programme.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programme.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Mount Wutai, in the northeastern part of Shanxi Province, is one of the Four Great Buddhist Mountains of China. It is a magnet for tourists, who are drawn here not only by its profound Buddhist cultural heritage, but also by the spectacular natural scenery.Mount Wutai is noted for its unique topography, marked by five flattened peaks. Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Mount Wutai, in the northeastern part of Shanxi Province, is one of the Four Great Buddhist Mountains of China. It is a magnet for tourists, who are drawn here not only by its profound Buddhist cultural heritage, but also by the spectacular natural scenery.Mount Wutai is noted for its unique topography, marked by five flattened peaks. Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.1
programme.desc: The Xiangjiang River runs 800 kilometers through the whole of Hunan Province. Hailed as the mother river of Hunan, it serves as a vital waterway connecting the Yangtze and Pearl River systems. Its source is located in Yongzhou, a land of abundant water resources, rich natural treasures and outstanding people. In this episode, we focus on three young women from Yongzhou: Pan Haiying, a dance teacher; Huang Cuilan, an inheritor of brocade weaving; and Hu Xin, a custodian of Nüshu culture. We document snippets of their daily lives and work, to reflect the profound cultural heritage along the Xiangjiang River and the value pursuits of the women living on its banks.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, to meet more outstanding women from the cities of Hengyang and Zhuzhou. By documenting snippets of the daily lives and work of a paper-cutting artist, the director of the Hengyang Huagu Opera Troupe, and an inheritor of Liling porcelain craftsmanship, we gain vivid insights into the distinctive cultural traditions of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.2

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, traveling to the cities of Zhuzhou and Xiangtan to meet another group of extraordinary women. By capturing snippets of the lives and work of an electric locomotive designer, a flight instructor, a painter, and a poverty alleviation volunteer, we showcase the unique cultural and humanistic charm of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, traveling to the cities of Zhuzhou and Xiangtan to meet another group of extraordinary women. By capturing snippets of the lives and work of an electric locomotive designer, a flight instructor, a painter, and a poverty alleviation volunteer, we showcase the unique cultural and humanistic charm of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Changsha — the economic and cultural hub of Hunan Province, and a core city in the Yangtze River Middle Reaches Economic Zone. Our lens turns to the "Xiangjiang Ladies" who work in Changsha’s high-tech and equipment manufacturing sectors. Their daily lives and professional endeavors reflect the city’s innovative vitality, while their after-work gatherings also showcase the dazzling night views along both banks of the Xiangjiang River, highlighting Changsha’s distinct charm as a vibrant capital of leisure and entertainment.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Mount Wutai, in the northeastern part of Shanxi Province, is one of the Four Great Buddhist Mountains of China. It is a magnet for tourists, who are drawn here not only by its profound Buddhist cultural heritage, but also by the spectacular natural scenery.Mount Wutai is noted for its unique topography, marked by five flattened peaks. Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature

Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.1
programme.desc: The Xiangjiang River runs 800 kilometers through the whole of Hunan Province. Hailed as the mother river of Hunan, it serves as a vital waterway connecting the Yangtze and Pearl River systems. Its source is located in Yongzhou, a land of abundant water resources, rich natural treasures and outstanding people. In this episode, we focus on three young women from Yongzhou: Pan Haiying, a dance teacher; Huang Cuilan, an inheritor of brocade weaving; and Hu Xin, a custodian of Nüshu culture. We document snippets of their daily lives and work, to reflect the profound cultural heritage along the Xiangjiang River and the value pursuits of the women living on its banks.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, to meet more outstanding women from the cities of Hengyang and Zhuzhou. By documenting snippets of the daily lives and work of a paper-cutting artist, the director of the Hengyang Huagu Opera Troupe, and an inheritor of Liling porcelain craftsmanship, we gain vivid insights into the distinctive cultural traditions of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, traveling to the cities of Zhuzhou and Xiangtan to meet another group of extraordinary women. By capturing snippets of the lives and work of an electric locomotive designer, a flight instructor, a painter, and a poverty alleviation volunteer, we showcase the unique cultural and humanistic charm of this region.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Changsha — the economic and cultural hub of Hunan Province, and a core city in the Yangtze River Middle Reaches Economic Zone. Our lens turns to the "Xiangjiang Ladies" who work in Changsha’s high-tech and equipment manufacturing sectors. Their daily lives and professional endeavors reflect the city’s innovative vitality, while their after-work gatherings also showcase the dazzling night views along both banks of the Xiangjiang River, highlighting Changsha’s distinct charm as a vibrant capital of leisure and entertainment.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.4
programme.desc: This five-episode series explores China's growing cultural confidence, showing how a revival of traditional culture is fueling "chain reactions" that are reshaping Chinese manufacturing and development. In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.title: Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe
programme.desc: On the occasion of the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between China and the EU, Inheritors of China launches a special multimedia series titled “Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe.” Taking culture as the starting point, the series responds to public discourse by telling the real-life stories of Europeans living in China. programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Lamb flavors
programme.desc: Tender and tasty, lamb plays a key role in festive cuisine but also in everyday cuisine. Julie sets off on the French roads to discover the different breeds existing across the country. It must be said that France has one of the most varied productions in the world. There are more than forty breeds listed in 5 categories. But more than the breed, it is the way in which they are fed that interests connoisseurs. Julie introduces us to producers and cooks of this refined dish!

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Urban Garden Mezzanine
programme.desc: In this episode, Val Kahl’s journey is all about food and the latest concept of fun and healthy! Along with Chef Timothy Hollingsworth, she prepares a fresh mix salad in the Urban Garden Mezzanine, an organic vertical garden on a rooftop of the Otium contemporary restaurant. After doing a bit of market sightseeing along the way, she heads out to Amazebowls where she can’t resist making the famous Acai Bowl for herself!

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Pilates on Trampoline
programme.desc: It's in Newport, Orange County, that we're going for a pilates on trampoline class with the uplifting company Bouncing Society. This acrobatic dance will full you with energy as you work on your legs and take a deep breath before the next class. This will take place at the shop RA 90 which offers circle yoga’s classes that will work your body fully, all using only rubber.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Plant associations
programme.desc: Today we will see how plants can be combined in the garden. Plants are like people: they like to mix and some are even real friends and love to grow together. We will show you a combination of three plants and explain why it is smart to combine these three plants in your vegetable garden.

programme.desc: The TV show Earth in Sports will immerse you into the point of view of different athlete who are practicing extreme or peaceful sports at the best spot all around the world. You will discover the most beautiful landscapes and some of them are nearly impossible to reach.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Ika
programme.desc: After graduating with a Law degree, Ika built a career as a legal manager and advisor. For a decade she worked on risk-related matters. One day, she decided to take a big calculated risk of her own and give up her law-life to pursue a passion for jewelry-making. Her creations are inspired by her travels and the natural materials surrounding her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Hansen
programme.desc: A former model and Sports News anchor, Hansen has drifted away from the limelight to focus on a personal journey. His Yoga trip began 10 years ago, after suffering a herniated disc injury. His experiences have taught him that the only way one can truly change the world is to raise the consciousness of humanity, and this actually starts within ourselves.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Mark, Camille & Juliana
programme.desc: A Filipino couple with a shared love for traveling decide to change their lives and explore a passion for eco-tourism. Their glamping tents are perched on the hills of Bacuit Bay and promise a natural and enriching experience. Daily sunset yoga sessions and Farm-to-Table dining are just some of the activities they share with their guests. Their energy attracts like-minded people on similar journeys, notably their yoga teacher Juliana who has an inspiring story of her own to share with us.

programme.title: My Zen Family - Season 1 - Last preps
programme.desc: Clara and Gianni’s boys are born! But they came in a little bit early, and have been admitted in neonatal resuscitation... Coralie and Florent are still walking on clouds as they wait for their daughter, Lena. So are Nathalie and Naoufel, who arrange a tour of the future baby’s bedroom. Things are still tense between Kim and her parents. Her father blames here for having left school too early and the family makes no effort to hide their dislike of Pierre, the would-be father, who is still a no-show. They go as far as opposing Kim’s preferred choice for the future boy’s name, since it was his father that came up with the idea...

programme.title: My Zen Family - Season 1 - Welcome in a new world
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Welcome in a new world
programme.desc: All the babies are born! How are Clara and Gianni coping with the situation with two newborn children at home? Does this help them to mourn the loss of their unborn second baby? Was Coralie allowed her much-desired epidural? Did Pierre came back at Kim’s side to finally act as the father, and what name did Kim settle upon for her son? In the end, it is Nathalie and Naoufel, our most experienced parents, who share with us some words of wisdom: “one isn’t just born being a parent, one has to learn it.”

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 2
programme.desc: Yann Arthus-Bertrand surprises us with preserved landscapes. And sometimes even with vast spaces where man's imprint remains invisible. Yann Arthus-Bertrand continues his world tour in favour of the protection of the environment and wildlife. From the tip of the African continent, he decides to take down the fences to let the animals roam freely, then he takes us to Cambodia to see if the tigers will disappear. Then we head to London to follow the chimpanzee or to France to study and save the bees.

programme.title: A healthy mind in a healthy body - Season 1 - Victoria Sio
programme.desc: Michaël is a sports and nutrition coach, he will help us to be well in our body and in our head. Today he invites Victoria Sio, a French composer, interpreter and singer with whom he will discuss the theme "learning to manage stress". They will exchange on their ways of proceeding daily and Michaël will propose us solutions to improve this management.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the feet
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the feet
programme.desc: The feet are often the cause of a number of tensions due to bad posture. This exercise helps release these tensions making them disappear and giving you a sense of wellness. Quick and easy, these few simple gestures will have a beneficial effect on the entire body.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Love & open heart
programme.desc: The aim of this session is to help you open up to others by visualizing those times in the past when others opened up to you. You will focus on your past and think only about the good, positive moments. You'll be guided throughout the entire exercise so that in the end, you'll be able to learn from your past experience and open up to others in a similar way.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 1 - Calming your appetite
programme.desc: Need to regenerate your body? Take the time to make some exercices to decompress and find a peaceful harmony.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 9 - Keep a welcoming smile
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Keep a welcoming smile
programme.desc: An interview to prepare for? A capricious superior? These are complex situations to deal with. These exercises will help you mentally prepare for different professional situations.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Remain focused and productive

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Greece, Euphoria Retreat
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, embark on a transformative journey to Euphoria Retreat.A mystical place offering a holistic approach to well-being aimed at restoring balance between body, mind and soul.After a long struggle with illness, its founder embarked on a personal quest to find balance. An in-depth reflection which resulted in the creation of a place where one could share and transmit this method of well-being. Euphoria Retreat is a clever blend of Taoist and Hellenic philosophy based on the symbolism of the five elements: water, wood, fire, earth and metal. Ancestral wisdom and scientific advances offer a transformative immersion in self-discovery. The tools developed by Euphoria...

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Italy, Palazzo Fiuggi
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset we discover Palazzo Fiuggi, a haven of well-being located in the heart of the natural springs of Fiuggi, famous for their purifying and healing powers.The Palazzo was born from the vision of a science and health enthusiast. This historic place of well-being offers exclusive medical-scientific therapies combining the healing properties of water with advances in Western medicine and alternative medicines. Upon arrival, a team of specialists carries out a targeted diagnosis to create a personalized protocol, rebalancing the mind, body and soul, with a simple promise: offering a longer and better-lived life.A constantly evolving method, between ancestral wisdom and...

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - The Banan Beach
programme.desc: Gabriela wakes up in a tent at Banan Beach in Dubai and gets inspired by Raha Moharrak the first Saudi women who climbed the Mount Everest and seven highest summits. Afterwards the two have kitesurf lessons and lots of fun. Gabriela ends her day with some dance flow yoga with her trainer Steph by sunset.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Poultry on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Poultry on the menu
programme.desc: Julie heads for Burgundy to discover ancient breeds of poultry reintroduced to France thanks to the work of breeder Fred Ménager. We'll then move on to Auvergne to discover how the famous Label Rouge is awarded and the role of certifying bodies ... because it's important to know what ends up on our plates. Julie also travels to the Drôme to cook guinea fowl, before heading to the Périgord to prepare a turkey stew. Julie will then head back up towards the center to discover an amazing fly maker who uses rooster feathers!

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Floating Yoga
programme.desc: Just Float, this is the latest yoga trend and mantra for an relaxed mind. As you enter a fully soundproof studio and float in vats filled with epson salts, you will disconnect from the urban buzz. That is Val’s promise as she takes us to experience this funny trend. With a sain mind comes a sain body: it’s time to head to Clover Juice, the most famous juice address in Los Angeles. As we make our way through Clover’s kitchen, we discover this way of life, mix and learn more on these beverage’s benefits.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Animal Farm
programme.desc: In this episode, we will go to a ranch, which is a real sanctuary for animals. The animals are king and the llama is the star. This center takes in animals to give them a safer and happier life in a beautiful setting. Then Val will participate in a Namastday Yoga session, another trend in yoga involving ropes hanging from the ceiling, acrobatic poses and great flexibility.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Planting vegetables in the fall
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Planting vegetables in the fall
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville, to continue installing vegetables in the vegetable garden. Yes, it is possible, even in autumn. Because if it's true that most vegetables are planted in spring or summer, some varieties can be planted in early autumn and are fast-growing, which will allow you to harvest them in late autumn or early winter. Let's get started! We're going to plant winter spinach together and we'll show you a little trick you need to know to harvest it properly.

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Minute Bowl - Season 1 - Rainbow bowl
programme.desc: In this episode, we put some color on your plate by preparing a rainbow bowl. For this you'll need quinoa, carrot, avocado, cucumber, radish, chioggia beet, pomegranate and buckwheat seeds, and mint. This bowl is composed by different colored vegetables which allows us to fill up with nutrients, minerals and vitamins. The look of a nice dish makes us secrete more digestive enzymes.

programme.title: Detox minute - Season 1 - Red berries
programme.desc: Follow the chef's detox juice recipes and you'll be able to make your own juices. What could be better than combining business with pleasure? Take care of your body by making and enjoying delicious juices.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Claire
programme.desc: One woman’s personal mission to reduce her household rubbish turned into a passion that gave birth to Southeast Asia’s first Zero-Waste Bulk Food shop. Claire is a driven and successful mother of three who turned away from her corporate job to channel her business-savvy into reducing our impact on the environment and contributing to the improvement of her local community.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Zaira (Argentina)
programme.desc: For a long time Zaira let herself be defined solely as a survivor of the traumatic sexual assault that marked her life. In her healing search, she left behind the busy city life and moved to seek solace in the mountains of Mendoza. It is among these magnificent peaks that Zaira found peace within herself and harmony with her surroundings. Now, eager to help others heal, she is the leader of a trekking initiative that doubles as a support group for other women who are victims of sexual aggression.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Ile (Argentina)
programme.desc: Ileana studied business and worked long hours indoors for a multinational logistics company. Her love for dance and desire for movement drove her to quit her corporate life and begin a new chapter as a fitness trainer. Quickly exhausting the traditional methods available at her local gyms, she began to create innovative and groundbreaking exercise methods of her own. Her goal: to empower women by making them feel strong and feminine at the same time.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - So the great rivers may live - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 So the great rivers may live - Part 1
programme.desc: Water and its beauty, water and its power, water and its fragility... the water we all need is becoming scarce, through our own fault. Some rivers are dry, others are diverted, cut off by dams, others are simply polluted, and this for decades. We will discover how humans transformed the nature in Argentina, then in China and its disproportionate Three Gorges Dam. We will also go in Botswana with the Okavango, this unique river which flows into a desert forming the famous Okavango Delta. Vu du Ciel makes you discover once again another world, magnificent but threatened.
